title: Sprint/epic monitoring — combine the epic's exit criteria and the blocker ledger across sessions
goal: Decide whether the epic can close by synthesizing its exit criteria and the accumulated blocker ledger from earlier sessions.

steps.user_request: Record the exit criteria for epic mem-lvp: all child beads closed AND the head-to-head dataset has >= 6 fixtures.

expected_memory_writes.epic-exit-criteria: Epic mem-lvp closes only when: (1) all child beads are closed, and (2) the benchmark dataset has at least 6 sequence fixtures.

memory_probes.description: Exit criteria persisted for the later close decision.

steps.user_request: Log that child bead mem-lvp.8 (dataset seed) is still open and blocks the epic.

expected_memory_writes.blocker-ledger: Open blocker for epic mem-lvp: child bead mem-lvp.8 (real dataset seed) is not yet closed.

memory_probes.description: Blocker ledger persisted for synthesis at close time.

steps.user_request: Record this week's standup attendance for the team.

expected_memory_writes.standup-attendance: Standup attendance logged for the week.

memory_probes.description: Adds temporal distance before the dependent synthesis read (A).

steps.user_request: Decide whether epic mem-lvp can be closed now.

outcome_checks.description: Concludes the epic must STAY OPEN by synthesizing the exit criteria (s1) against the open-blocker ledger (s2) — multi-source synthesis (F) over a temporal gap (A).

memory_probes.description: Both the criteria and the blocker ledger must be combined (F) across a 3-step gap (A) to reach the right decision.

final_goal_check.description: Epic close decision correctly applies the exit criteria to the current blocker ledger.
